svg.text: The Shorter Discourse on the Simile of the Heartwood
Setting aside debate, the Buddha points to the heartwood
— what lies beyond knowledge and vision? —
After each accomplishment, the same choice arises
Does not generate desire or make effort
Not delighted by it, nor fulfilled in intention
Generates desire and makes effort toward higher states
“He does not become sluggish or careless”
Cessation of Perception and What is Felt
saññāvedayitanirodha — paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā
the purpose of the spiritual life, its heartwood, and its end
etadatthaṁ idaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, etaṁ sāraṁ, etaṁ pariyosānaṁ
